characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Glowing Eyes of Doom: His eyes are shown gleaming white in Sir Pentious's flashback. Karma Houdini: Since Sir Pentious never reported him, he faced no consequences (at least, no earthly ones) and killed five more women. No Name Given: Sir Pentious knows he was a wealthy and powerful individual but his specific name isn't given. That said, it's heavily implied that he'sbased onthe historicJack the Ripper, given that he was active in London during the 1880s, murdered exclusively women and was never caught. Screw the Rules, I Have Money!: He is stated to be a rich and powerful individual that also happens to be Sir Pentious' client. Pentious' reluctance of reporting him to the authorities was likely due to fear of retribution from offending a man of such wealth and influence. Small Role, Big Impact: Sir Pentious believes that him not reporting the killer's identity out of cowardice was what damned him to Hell, and by proxyallowed him to be the first sinner to redeem himself and enter Heaven.

characters.name: Alastor's Victim
characters.description: Seen in "It's a Deal". A high society type in New Orleans whom Alastor murdered after he had spilled wine on the future Radio Demon's sleeve. Asshole Victim: Whilehis murder is horrific, and it's ambiguous whether the first time he stains Alastor is on purpose or by accident, the way he laughs at Alastor after realizing he did it and thenkeepsspilling his drink on him does not endear him to any viewers for the few seconds he's onscreen and no one in the audience mourns him. Casting Gag:Brandon Rogersis best known for voicinga professional hitmanwithin the Hellaverse, so having him also voice a murder victim (who is in line withthe hitman's usual kinds of targets, no less) makes for some irony. Never Found the Body: Alastor kept his corpse. From the state of it, Alastor had been holding it long enough for it to decompose into a near-skeleton. Upper-Class Twit: His tuxedo and careless handling of wine suggest he's fairly affluent.

characters.name: Alastor's Killer
characters.description: Accidental Hero: Sure, his shooting of Alastor wasAccidental Murder, but hedidend up stopping a cannibalisticSerial Killerfrom continuing his spree.In life at least. Accidental Murder: As stated above, the hunter killing Alastor was due to mistaking him for a deer while he was dragging a body through the woods at night. Ambiguous Situation: Alastor died the day aftermaking a deal with Rosie.Rosieherself not only knew, but rubbed the coincidence in Alastor's face, hinting that this may have in fact been by her design. The Faceless: His face is not shown, and he's only seen from waist down. The Spook: A complete mystery to everyone, except maybeRosie.

characters.name: Vincent's Victims
characters.description: Seen in "Brighter" from "Weapon of Mass Distraction". Various television presenters of a station whom Vincent Whittman (later Vox) had murdered so he could ascend the media ladder. They include a news anchor, a late-night talk show host, a pair of producers, a zoo show host, a cooking show hostess, a game show host, and the network's CEO, Robert "Bob" Sinclaire. Bludgeoned to Death: Vincent killed the talk show host by repeatedly bashing one of the talk show's set lamps on him. Cruel and Unusual Death: Most of Vincent's kills were brutal, but the ones that particularly take the cake are those of the producers (both strangled and then hanged from a microphone cord) and Bob (thrown off the network buildingafterbeing stabbed andwhileelectrocuted by lightning). Desecrating the Dead: Vincent not only killed the news anchor to take his job, he also danced with his corpse and left it in a dumpster in an alleyway. Harmful to Minors: At least one little girl was present and witnessed Bob's charred corpse hit the pavement.

characters.name: Eve
characters.description: Did They or Didn't They?: Lucifer's comments in the Season 1 finale seem to imply that he not only married Lilith, he may have also seduced Eve at some point as well. Though it's not clear if he's merely mocking theOriginal Manor not, and it's also possible he was simply referring tooffering Eve the Forbidden Fruit. Given that Adam is aPsychopathic ManchildandPolitically Incorrect Villain, andhas been since the dawn of creation,can you really blame her if she did? Missing Mom: She's essentially the "Mother of Humanity", but hasn't made an appearance. Second Love: Was this to Adam after Lilith ditched him for being controlling. Slasher Smile: In Charlie's story, after Eve takes the Fruit of Knowledge, she's shown sporting aPsychotic Smirkthat grows into a terrifying red-tinted smile—possibly implying that either given freedom had corrupted her, or there was already something wrong with her before eating the fruit. Small Role, Big Impact: Her current role is in a flashback in a story Charlie told. However, her eating the fruit allowed evil to enter Earth and cause Lucifer's banishment to Hell.
